economic_notes: Band operates Adams Lake Development Corporation and ALIB Construction Group Inc. as consolidated subsidiaries. Timber harvesting and natural resources are significant activities. Forest and Range Consultation and Revenue Sharing Agreement (FCRSA) funds held. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP investment. First Nations Sales Tax collection agreement in place. Ministry of Forests fine of approximately $300,000 under negotiation. Environmental contingency related to Woods Division in Adams Lake Development Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2021. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Adams Lake Development Corporation and ALIB Construction Group Inc. Segmented appendix data was cut off in the provided text so departmental expense breakdowns could not be extracted. ISC funding per revenue line is $6,430,112 which equates to 31% of gross revenues per Note A20. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($6,430,112) + CMHC ($330,608) + Province of BC ($3,001,445) + First Nations Health Authority ($1,395,560) = $11,157,725. Demand loan of $28,963 plus mortgages of $1,096,127 constitute long-term debt. Annual debt service of $237,455 from cash flow statement financing activities debt reduction. Silviculture liability of $2,333,319 is a significant non-standard liability. Investments balance is negative ($247,387) due to equity method drawings exceeding contributions in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP. Prior year auditor was Daley & Company CPA LLP (partners joined Grant Thornton January 4, 2021).
